dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Why do you look at one another?

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: Behold, I have heard that there is grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy grain for us there, that we may live and not die

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Where do you come from?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: From the land of Canaan, to buy food

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: You are spies; you have come to see the nakedness of the land

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: No, my lord, your servants have come to buy food

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men; your servants have never been spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: No, it is the nakedness of the land that you have come to see

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We, your servants, are twelve brothers, the sons of one man in the land of Canaan; and behold, the youngest is this day with our father, and one is no more

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: It is as I said to you: you are spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: By this you shall be tested: by the life of Pharaoh, you shall not go from this place unless your youngest brother comes here

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Send one of you, and let him bring your brother, while you remain confined, that your words may be tested, whether there is truth in you. Or else, by the life of Pharaoh, surely you are spies

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do this and you will live, for I fear God

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers remain confined where you are in custody, and let the rest go and carry grain for the famine of your households

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: And bring your youngest brother to me, so your words will be verified, and you shall not die

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: In truth we are guilty concerning our brother, in that we saw the distress of his soul when he begged us and we did not listen. That is why this distress has come upon us

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Did I not tell you not to sin against the boy? But you did not listen. So now there comes a reckoning for his blood

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: My money has been put back; here it is in my sack!

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: What is this that God has done to us?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: The man, the lord of the land, spoke roughly to us and took us to be spies of the land

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: But we said to him, 'We are honest men; we have never been spies'

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: We are twelve brothers, sons of our father; one is no more, and the youngest is this day with our father in the land of Canaan

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Then the man, the lord of the land, said to us, 'By this I shall know that you are honest men: leave one of your brothers here with me, and take grain for the famine of your households and go'

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: And bring your youngest brother to me, then I shall know that you are not spies but honest men, and I will deliver your brother to you, and you shall trade in the land

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: You have bereaved me of my children: Joseph is no more, and Simeon is no more, and now you would take Benjamin. All this has come against me

dialogue.speaker: reuben
dialogue.englishText: Kill my two sons if I do not bring him back to you. Put him in my hands, and I will bring him back to you

dialogue.speaker: jacob
dialogue.englishText: My son shall not go down with you, for his brother is dead, and he is the only one left. If harm should happen to him on the journey that you are to make, you would bring down my gray hairs with sorrow to Sheol
